methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $400M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Elroy Air, a Series B autonomous cargo drone company, is valued at $400M post-money. The Drones & Autonomous sector includes 7 private peers (Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Manna Aero, Asylon Robotics among others) and no direct public comparables, limiting traditional peer benchmarking.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable method here. Using Elroy's $400M Series B post-money valuation as the base and applying a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier for progression to the next funding stage yields $760M. This reflects typical venture capital markup patterns in autonomous systems at this maturity level. Peer-median valuation could not be applied: while three same-sector companies exist, none met the filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier comparables with sufficient sample size (minimum three datapoints required). A sector-wide median would introduce unacceptable heterogeneity.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $646M to $874M, with a midpoint of $760M. This represents meaningful upside from the current $400M valuation. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-band peer trading multiples—critical given the fragmented competitive landscape—and the early-stage maturity of the autonomous cargo sector itself, where unit economics and regulatory pathways remain unproven at scale. The single-method derivation also warrants caution; results should be stress-tested against any future peer funding events or public market comps.
